title: Caltech Workshop Reproducibility Handout (from CodeOcean, Addgene, protocols.io)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9h5qs","text":"This is a handout that was created by Code Ocean, Addgene, and protocols.io for the Caltech workshop on reproducibility. For more information and slides from the workshop, please see: https://codeocean.com/workshop/caltech.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":184,"length":38}],"data":[]},{"key":"f0bjh","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1r2nq","text":"Please feel free to clone and modify it. guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"828ts","text":"Practical tips for reproducibility","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8e6m9","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"99nig","text":"1. Plan for reproducibility before you start","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3o1kk","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8taus","text":"Write a study plan or protocol and track new versions.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8rjlh","text":"Set-up a reproducible project using an electronic lab notebook to organize and track your work. Avoid saving proprietary file formats.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f828s","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f8q4f","text":"2. Keep track of things","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4mhjq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fm50g","text":"Preregister important study design and analysis information. Free tools to help you make your first registration include AsPredicted, Open Science Framework, and Registered Reports. Clinical trials use Clinicaltrials.gov.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":121,"length":11},{"key":1,"offset":134,"length":22},{"key":2,"offset":162,"length":18},{"key":3,"offset":202,"length":18}],"data":[]},{"key":"9otua","text":"Track changes to your files using version control.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8lm53","text":"Document everything done by hand in a README file and data dictionary. steps: Addgene https://www.addgene.org/ (nonprofit plasmid repository) CiteAb https://www.citeab.com/ (antibody search engine with results sorted by citations) ICLAC http://iclac.org/ (registry of false or misidentified cell lines) Quartzy https://www.quartzy.com/ (manage lab inventory)

Benchling https://benchling.com/ (free) Evernote https://evernote.com/ (most popular with biologists but not designed as an ELN) Labguru https://www.labguru.com/ ($) sciNote https://scinote.net/ (open source, free) Open Science Framework https://osf.io/ (free)

Bio-Protocol https://bio-protocol.org/ (A peer-reviewed protocol journal; free to read & publish) protocols.io http://protocols.io/ (an open access repository of science methods; free to read & publish)

Github https://github.com/ (code repository; free for public repos) Jupyter Notebooks http://jupyter.org/ (open source web-app for creating & sharing live code, equations, and more) Code Ocean https://codeocean.com/ (computational reproducibility platform; free to upload, share & publish executable code with DOI; pay for more computing time over freemium limit)

DataDryad http://datadryad.org/ (curated digital repository; free to access, $120 to publish dataset up to 20GB) Figshare http://figshare.com/ (free digital repository, 5GB per file limit) Zenodo https://zenodo.org/ (free digital repository; 50GB per dataset limit)
creator: Lenny Teytelman
